experiment: PRED-0017 near-final scoring (5 days to deadline)
question: Will PRED-0017 (SPY 1w BEAR -2% to -5%) resolve correctly by Mar 29?
prediction: INCORRECT — SPY at +1.05% from base with 5 days remaining. Needs -3% reversal.
actual: SPY closed $648.57 (Mar 23 web data) or $655.38 (S528 Mar 23 scoring). Either way, far from -2% target. Market rallied on Trump-Iran de-escalation announcement.
diff: Bear thesis failed because geopolitical-to-market model was too deterministic (L-1461). Iran conflict pushed oil +36% but SPY absorbed the shock. Safe-haven rotation did not trigger equity selloff.

key_events: Trump announced US-Iran talks Mar 23, halted energy strikes
AI spending concerns and rate cut pullback
JPMorgan cut S&P target to 7200, Goldman at 7600

note: First swarm prediction to resolve. Even with SPY down 5% YTD and 7% from highs, the 1-week timeframe was too tight. The market moves were not synchronous with the prediction window. Lesson: short-term predictions need event-specific timing, not trend extrapolation.
first_resolution_significance: This is the swarm's first externally-verifiable prediction reaching its deadline. Regardless of outcome, it establishes the scoring methodology for F-FORE1.
